access.guest_fallback_message: Detailed explanations are available for logged-in Saola students only.

q9.explanation: Câu 9 mâu thuẫn với thông tin ở đoạn 1. Bài nêu: "It was only after Gall’s death that Johanne Spurzheim, one of Gall’s students, labelled the idea “phrenology”. The idea first came to Gall when he noticed that university classmates who could memorise great amounts of information with relative ease seemed to have prominent eyes and large foreheads. He speculated that other internal qualities, besides memory, might be indicated by an external feature as well.". Câu hỏi đã đổi một chi tiết quan trọng hoặc đảo ngược ý trong bài, nên đáp án là FALSE.
q9.evidence: It was only after Gall’s death that Johanne Spurzheim, one of Gall’s students, labelled the idea “phrenology”. The idea first came to Gall when he noticed that university classmates who could memorise great amounts of information with relative ease seemed to have prominent eyes and large foreheads. He speculated that other internal qualities, besides memory, might be indicated by an external feature as well._

q10.explanation: Câu 10 khớp với thông tin ở đoạn 4. Ý chính trong bài là: "Spurzheim wrote that the premise of phrenology was to use the methods to identify individuals who stood out at both poles of society: those with a propensity for making important social contributions and those with a greater than normal tendency for evil. The former were to be encouraged, nurtured, and developed in order to maximise their potential for good. The latter needed to be curbed and segregated to protect society from their predisposition to harm others.". Vì câu hỏi diễn đạt lại cùng một ý, đáp án là TRUE.
q10.evidence: Spurzheim wrote that the premise of phrenology was to use the methods to identify individuals who stood out at both poles of society: those with a propensity for making important social contributions and those with a greater than normal tendency for evil. The former were to be encouraged, nurtured, and developed in order to maximise their potential for good. The latter needed to be curbed and segregated to protect society from their predisposition to harm others._

q11.explanation: Câu 11 có một số từ khóa gần với đoạn 5, nhưng bài không cung cấp đủ chi tiết để xác nhận hay phủ định toàn bộ mệnh đề. Câu gần nhất là: "Phrenology has met with a good deal of criticism since it was proposed, but over time it has also been credited for some aspects of its teachings. John Fancher, a critic of phrenology, states that it was a curious mixture, combining some keen observations and insights about the brain with an inappropriate scientific procedure. Most criticism is aimed at the poor methods used by phrenologists and the tangent from standard scientific procedure in investigating.". Vì thiếu thông tin trực tiếp, đáp án là NOT GIVEN.
q11.evidence: Phrenology has met with a good deal of criticism since it was proposed, but over time it has also been credited for some aspects of its teachings. John Fancher, a critic of phrenology, states that it was a curious mixture, combining some keen observations and insights about the brain with an inappropriate scientific procedure. Most criticism is aimed at the poor methods used by phrenologists and the tangent from standard scientific procedure in investigating._

q12.explanation: Câu 12 mâu thuẫn với thông tin ở đoạn 6. Bài nêu: "Pierre Flourens, a French physiologist, was also appalled by the shoddy methods of phrenologists and was determined to study the functions of the brain strictly by experiment. The specific technique used by Flourens, who was also a highly skilled surgeon, was ablation, the surgical removal of certain small parts of the brain. He ablated precisely determined portions of bird, rabbit, and dog brains and then observed the behaviour of his subjects.". Câu hỏi đã đổi một chi tiết quan trọng hoặc đảo ngược ý trong bài, nên đáp án là FALSE.
q12.evidence: Pierre Flourens, a French physiologist, was also appalled by the shoddy methods of phrenologists and was determined to study the functions of the brain strictly by experiment. The specific technique used by Flourens, who was also a highly skilled surgeon, was ablation, the surgical removal of certain small parts of the brain. He ablated precisely determined portions of bird, rabbit, and dog brains and then observed the behaviour of his subjects._

q13.explanation: Câu 13 có một số từ khóa gần với đoạn 4, nhưng bài không cung cấp đủ chi tiết để xác nhận hay phủ định toàn bộ mệnh đề. Câu gần nhất là: "Spurzheim, who studied under Gall, put a more metaphysical and philosophical spin on his teacher’s concept when he named it “phrenology”, meaning “science of the mind”. To Spurzheim, phrenology was the science that could tell people what they are and why they are who they are. Spurzheim wrote that the premise of phrenology was to use the methods to identify individuals who stood out at both poles of society: those with a propensity for making important social contributions and those with a greater than normal tendency for evil.". Vì thiếu thông tin trực tiếp, đáp án là NOT GIVEN.
q13.evidence: Spurzheim, who studied under Gall, put a more metaphysical and philosophical spin on his teacher’s concept when he named it “phrenology”, meaning “science of the mind”. To Spurzheim, phrenology was the science that could tell people what they are and why they are who they are. Spurzheim wrote that the premise of phrenology was to use the methods to identify individuals who stood out at both poles of society: those with a propensity for making important social contributions and those with a greater than normal tendency for evil._

q14.explanation: Câu 14 khớp với thông tin ở đoạn 7. Ý chính trong bài là: "In the same period, the discipline was also introduced to America, where it flourished. In fact, public interest in phrenology in Britain had waned considerably when the idea was reintroduced to the country by a pair of enterprising brothers from the United States. Although phrenology has long since been discredited, some of its basic principles, such as the idea that specific functions are localised in particular areas of the brain, have been borne out by more recent research.". Vì câu hỏi diễn đạt lại cùng một ý, đáp án là TRUE.
q14.evidence: In the same period, the discipline was also introduced to America, where it flourished. In fact, public interest in phrenology in Britain had waned considerably when the idea was reintroduced to the country by a pair of enterprising brothers from the United States. Although phrenology has long since been discredited, some of its basic principles, such as the idea that specific functions are localised in particular areas of the brain, have been borne out by more recent research._
